title: 50 Days: More than 500 Children / Fatalities in the 2014 Gaza conflict
pages_or_sections: Search snippet / report summary cited in web results
strengths: Best accessible status-classified fatality series in the source set.
limitations: The original B'Tselem page is no longer the easiest stable access path here; women-specific count is not as cleanly paired in the same extracted passage.
notes: Paraphrase: of 2,202 Palestinians killed by Israeli forces, 1,391 did not take part in hostilities, 526 of them were children, 765 took part in hostilities or had a continuous combat function, and 46 could not be classified.

title: Key Figures on the 2014 Hostilities
strengths: Strong humanitarian context and total-casualty cross-check.
limitations: Its civilian total differs from B'Tselem's tighter status-classified count.
notes: Paraphrase: OCHA reports 2,251 Palestinians killed, including 1,462 civilians, 551 children, and 299 women, plus 11,231 injured.

title: Population, Housing and Establishments Census – mid-2014 population press release
strengths: Official mid-year population benchmark for the war year.
limitations: Single mid-year point rather than daily war population.
notes: Paraphrase: the Gaza Strip population was about 1.76 million in mid-2014.

title: The 2014 Gaza Conflict: Hamas’s Crimes against Civilians and Israel’s Legal Response
strengths: Explicit theater-wide militant-force breakdown for the war period.
limitations: State source with litigation/advocacy purpose; used here only for the exposure denominator, not for death attribution.
notes: Paraphrase: militants operating in Gaza during the conflict were estimated at about 16,000 Hamas, 5,000 PIJ, and 5,000 other groups.

title: Key figures on the 2014 hostilities
pages_or_sections: Key figures bullets: 2,251 Palestinians killed, including 1,462 civilians; of Palestinian fatalities, 551 were children and 299 women.
strengths: Concise post-war summary tied to the Commission of Inquiry source base, with a whole-war civilian total and separate child/women counts.
limitations: The page does not itself print an adult-women age cutoff, so adult women are inferred from the separate child category plus standard OCHA wording.
notes: Primary fatality source for the bundle.

title: On the Occasion of the International Population Day 11/07/2014
pages_or_sections: Paragraph stating Gaza Strip population 1.76 million, including 894,000 males and 866,000 females.
strengths: Official Gaza-wide population and sex totals near the war year.
limitations: Does not provide the under-18 share needed for child denominators.
notes: Used for total and female exposure baselines.

title: On the Occasion of the Palestinian Child's Day 04/04/2014
pages_or_sections: Paragraph stating that children under 18 constituted 45.1% in Palestine and 50.6% in Gaza Strip in 2013.
strengths: Official under-18 share specific to Gaza and close in time to the war.
limitations: The share is reported for 2013 rather than mid-2014.

title: On the Occasion of the Palestinian Child's Day, 04/04/2024
pages_or_sections: Paragraph giving Gaza children under 18 as 544,776 males and 523,210 females.
date_scope: Mid-2024 child sex split used as an assumption aid
strengths: Gaza-specific child sex split for deriving adult female and adult male shares.
limitations: Later than the war year; used only as an auxiliary split, not as a direct war-year population count.
notes: Supports derivation of adult population shares matched to the adult-women death category.
